content: Are you referring to Thunderbird marking a message as Read after selecting it? The setting that controls that behavior is found at <Settings | General | Reading & Display | "Automatically mark messages as read">. You can set this for a specific length of time or turn it off entirely, in which case the message will remain marked as Unread until you manually change it (you can use the M key to toggle that state.)

content:
These steps should work if thunderbird is installed in same location on the new PC:

  1. start thunderbird on old PC
  2. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  3. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  4. exit thunderbird - you should now be in the profile folder. You can verify that by looking to see if abook.sqlite is there.
  5. copy profile content to external media
  6. install thunderbird on new pc, downloaded from thunderbird.net
  7. start thunderbird
  8. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  9. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  10. exit thunderbird
  11. delete everthing there
  12. now, paste contents of exported profile
  13. if there is a compatibility.ini file, delete it
  14. restart thunderbird

content:
The inability to write to the mail file, which is essentially what that error is about, usually means some other process is using the file at the time it is to be written.

So the first thing to check is the Anti-Virus. What Anti-Virus are you using? Exit Thunderbird Access the Anti-virus and make your Thunderbird profile folder exempt from scanning.

If you downloaded Thunderbird from the official website: https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

Then, it's likely your Thunderbird folder to exempt will be here: C://Users/username/Appdata/Roaming/Thunderbird

If you exempt that folder then it will also exempt anything it contains.

Restart Thunderbird and report on results.

content:
Thunderbird is a free program. There are no people sat in call centres. All monies are spent on the product. They do provide this Thunderbird Support Forum for those needing some help. Thunderbird relies on donations. The people who try to offer Help via this Support Forum are all thunderbird users who offer their free time as unpaid volunteers. But I can reassure you they are all very real people and not bots.

When you post a question it's useful to always include some additional useful information such as:

Q: What OS are you using ? I expect it's a MAC but not sure which one.

Q: What version the Thunderbird are you using? Q: Did you get the download from this official website? https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/

You say "Unable to set up new account using setup wizard" Unable because of what... Q: Do you get an error message ? It would help if you could upload some images that show the error and server settings. I could be guessing what problem you encountered until xmas :)

Q: Have you logged on to the webmail account via a browser and checked to see if IMAP and or POP have been enabled for use?

Q: Are you able to logon to the webmail account? Please confirm you can do this as it will confirm that password works.

Q: Are you trying to create an IMAP or a POP Mail account?

Q: Are you using 'Xfinity' ? If yes, then have you logged on to webmail account - Settings > security and selected the checkbox to allow Third party access to server ?

Q: Are you trying to set up a mail account that uses Yahoo or Gmail or outlook servers ? If yes, then they all use Oauth2 for Authentication, so this requires several settings set up.

check the following and confirm you have done all the following: 1. Firewall - Access Firewall and set up Thunderbird as an allowed program to stop your computer blocking it.

2. VPN - If you have a VPN that has been enabled then switch it off because most servers do not like VPN as it hides your IP address and they may block you because they think you are a hacker. Alternative create a split tunnelling for Thunderbird to use.

3. Localhost - Q: Do you use anything like 'Apache' or any other product that is using the localhost port? If yes, then switch it off until Oauth2 token has been set up.

Q: Are cookies enabled ? In Thunderbird

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • select 'Accept cookies from sites'

Q: What server are you trying to connect to? Q: What comes after the @ in your email address as that might help to work out what server settings you need to use.

Some servers now insist that whilst a normal password gets access to webmail, they insist users generate an app specific password for use in a third party app eg: Thunderbird email client. So, if I know more then I can advise whether that is required.

Please keep all responses in this Forum Question.

content:
re :import from one email account to another

If people have two email address eg: one in outlook and one in gmail

If they want to accumulate all emails in gmail, then this is usually done via the webmail accounts. Basically forwarding all emails from one server to another. Then in Thunderbird, you would just have the gmail account set up and you would see all email. Both those sent direct to gmail address and all those forwarded from the outlook server to gmail server.

But you say "makes me have same mail in 2 places" so I'm trying to think what might cause that sort of view.

Have you set up a 'Message Filter' to 'Copy' emails from one account to another? The would be in 'Tools' > 'Message Filters' Look for a filter that copies all emails to another account Inbox. Either disable it or delete it.

I notice that previously you asked a question and said you had two email address using 'suddenlink', both set up as Pop accounts. I presume you had set them up as normal mail accounts so each account was shown in Folder Pane and each account had it's own set of folders.

I'm wondering if you have not 'imported' nor 'copied' anything, but are seeing a set up that would display each account Inbox separately and also all in one place in a virtual folder like a Unified Inbox. Did you enable the 'Unified View' in the Folder Pane ?

  • 'View' > 'Folders' > You normally would have 'All folders' selected, but have you got 'Unified Folders' selected ?

If yes, then click on the 'Unified Folders' option to remove the selection and go back to a standard view of Folder Pane.

content:
I can only guess. because you apparently have no idea what you have done and are unable to offer any indication of where to start, but there are only really two possibilities.

1. You directed your mail from Suddenlink into Yahoo. Optimum offer instructions for that here https://myemail.suddenlink.net/webmail/help/richui-English/Ux%20Suite%20Online%20Help/Settings/Mail%20options/Setting%20up%20Auto-forward.htm

2. You asked Yahoo to retrieve your Suddenlink mail. Yahoo offers instructions on how to do that and how to undo that here https://au.help.yahoo.com/kb/SLN36713.html

Thunderbird does offer unified folders that merge mail from multiple accounts into a unified inbox, but that is not placing one account into another it is a discreet folder for say inbox that merges the content of all configured accounts into a global folder and offers the individual account as a sub folder. AN example is shown below.

The more traditional "All folders" view represents those same accounts this way.

Which view or views you use is controlled from the following menu

I have no idea if you have merged your accounts as you initially said using your service providers tools, or are only referring to the way the information of presented in Thunderbird. It is up to you to make that call no one but you has access to either your Thunderbird or your mail accounts, so you are going to have to work it out.

content:
Use 'Match any' instead of 'Match all'. Trying to fight spam using static filters is futile. Using TB built-in spam mail controls, and training the filter for what's spam, and what's not spam is your best bet. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/thunderbird-and-junk-spam-messages

Note, unless spam messages already get blocked on your email provider's server, once they are received by Thunderbird, all your filter can do is to move them to the Spam folder, so that your Inbox will not get clogged with spam.

content: I suggest making the folder focus just on the minimum, such as the domain name, e/g.. @xxxx and avoid the full email address. And I think you want filter for ANY, not ALL. That is, one of your filters i for omaha AND steak AND marriott. Do you really require all three words in the subject? Or any one of them? Your other filter requires the FROM to be from bluecross AND from marriot AND from Ace And from Club - that will never be true. You probably meant ANY, not ALL.
